human_verification.notes: Reviewed issue #1251 source expectations, boundary controls, scenario coverage, functionality mapping, deployment/commercialization notes, and runtime strategy for specification-based posture.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to data center execution triage, access verification, interface dependency handling, exception routing, and completion-evidence capture.
Manifest authority sources cover cloud computing terminology, incident handling, access and control standards, and resilience rollback expectations with refresh and review metadata.
Research summary maps workflow stages, artifacts, systems of record, decision boundaries, and domain failure modes while retaining no blocking unresolved ambiguities.

Evaluation scenarios (dco-01 to dco-10) cover normal execution, missing evidence, conflict handling, authority boundary refusal, adjacent handoffs, testing/rollback gaps, low-confidence routing, and rework completion.
delegated_meta_agent_evidence: Commercial packaging wording and periodic source-refresh recommendations can be periodically aligned with shared market-readiness tooling without reducing this package's boundary safety model.
No additional delegated authority is required for deployment claim at this stage due to explicit specification-based runtime posture.
notes: validated evidence supports a bounded data-center execution specialist that remains runtime `specification-based` and defers retained-implementation claims until an explicit promotion commit.
No unreviewed regressions were added during local package creation; scenario and function coverage are complete and cross-industry required source classes are represented.
